auth.loginDescription: Welcome back. Use your administrator account to access the dashboard.
auth.emailHelp: Use the administrator email that has been granted access.
auth.passwordHelp: Password must be at least 6 characters.

chat.title: Chat Playground
chat.pageDescription: An AI Elements-style chat surface powered by the current streaming backend endpoint.
chat.workspacePageDescription: Chat against a selected workspace to test agent and orchestration behavior with the same streaming surface.
chat.formDescription: Send a streaming request to `/api/chat/completions` using your current authenticated session.
chat.targetRequired: Select an agent team or direct agent before sending.
chat.workspaceHelp: Pick the workspace whose agents and orchestration runtime should answer this chat.
chat.workspaceRequired: Select a workspace before sending a workspace chat request.
chat.agentHelp: Route directly to this agent, bypassing workspace orchestration.
chat.modelHelp: Use any model identifier supported by the active provider.
chat.systemPromptHelp: Changing the model or system prompt starts a fresh thread on the next send.
chat.promptPlaceholder: Ask a question, request a summary, or sketch a plan...
chat.conversationDescription: Messages are rendered in a chat-first layout while still using the current backend stream contract.
chat.sidebarDescription: Browse previous conversations and jump back into an existing thread.
chat.settingsDescription: Tune the active model and assistant behavior before you send the next prompt.
chat.emptyDescription: The current backend already supports streaming and conversation IDs, so this view now behaves more like a real chat thread.
chat.continueDescription: New prompts continue the current conversation until you clear the thread or change the session settings.
chat.startDescription: The first prompt creates a conversation and stores the returned conversation ID for the next turns.
chat.threadConnectedDescription: The backend returned a conversation ID. New prompts will continue this thread.
chat.threadIdleDescription: No conversation ID yet. Your next prompt will start a fresh thread.
chat.outputDescription: Assistant output is streamed chunk by chunk from the backend.
chat.emptyState: No response yet. Submit a prompt to test the chat completions API.
chat.traceDescription: Inspect the run summary and individual orchestration steps for this assistant reply.
chat.traceEmpty: Select an assistant reply to inspect its execution trace.
chat.traceLoadFailed: Could not load the run trace.
chat.traceNoSteps: No steps were recorded for this run.
chat.traceFlowDescription: Trace the path the reply took across orchestrator and worker agents.
chat.traceStepSelected: Select any node in the flow to inspect its payload and result.

suggestions.greeting: Write a short Vietnamese greeting for a new user.
suggestions.summary: Summarize the latest frontend conventions in 5 bullets.
suggestions.plan: Draft a rollout plan for adding image upload to chat.

page.title: Runtime logs
page.description: Inspect backend logs with quick filters for date, level, trace ID, and message text.

filters.title: Filters
filters.description: Narrow the current log file before reading or copying lines.

results.title: Log output
results.descriptionEmpty: No log file is configured for reading yet.

logs.empty: No log lines matched the current filters.

codes.validation_error: Please review the highlighted input and try again.
codes.channel_already_exists: A channel with this name already exists.
codes.channel_encryption_failed: Channel credentials could not be encrypted.
codes.channel_test_connection_unsupported_kind: This channel type does not support connection testing.
codes.channel_test_connection_telegram_auth_fa: Telegram bot token is invalid or could not be verified.
codes.channel_test_connection_discord_auth_fai: Discord bot token is invalid or could not be verified.
codes.channel_test_connection_slack_auth_faile: Slack bot token is invalid or could not be verified.
codes.channel_test_connection_slack_socket_mod: Slack app token is invalid or Socket Mode could not be opened.

page.title: LLM providers
page.description: Manage provider records, API keys, and runtime defaults for agents and chat.

providers.searchPlaceholder: Search by provider name, model, or base URL
providers.emptyProviders: No providers match the current search.

table.selectAll: Select all providers on this page
table.name: Name

form.name: Provider name
form.apiKeyOptionalPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep the current key
form.apiKeyHelp: The API key is required when creating a provider.
form.apiKeyCodexHelp: Codex can be created without an API key. Leave this blank if your environment already provides access.
form.apiKeyUpdateHelp: Leave this blank if you want to keep the current encrypted API key.
form.activeHelp: Use this provider for live chat requests.
form.primaryHelp: Mark this provider as the default option.

sheet.createDescription: Register a new LLM provider for runtime chat requests.
sheet.editDescription: Adjust model settings or update the API key for this provider.

form.apiKeyOptionalPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep the current key

form.primaryHelp: Mark this credential as the default credential for this provider.

errors.loadCredentials: Could not load the provider credential list.

form.name: Agent name

form.llmProviderHelp: Choose which provider this agent should use by default.
form.providersHelp: The first provider is primary. Additional providers are used as fallbacks in priority order.
form.providersNotRequired: Providers are only required for LLM agents.
form.modelOverrideHelp: Leave blank to use the provider default model.
form.systemPromptPlaceholder: Describe the agent behavior, tone, and constraints.
form.parentAgentHelp: Optional. Use this when the agent belongs under another agent.
form.metadataJsonHelp: Optional JSON metadata stored with the agent.

page.description: Manage markdown skill files that agents can attach and execute during orchestration.

table.selectAll: Select all skills on this page
table.name: Name

form.name: Skill name
form.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe what this skill should help the agent do.
form.fileNameHelp: Use a `.md` filename because the backend stores the skill as a markdown file.

form.uploadFileUpdate: Drag and drop a markdown file to replace this skill, or click to choose one
form.uploadFileHelp: Selecting a file only attaches it to the form. Click save to create, update, or import.
form.uploadFileCta: Create supports .md or .zip. Edit supports only .md.
form.contentPlaceholder:

Explain how the agent should search internal docs.
form.contentHelp: The markdown body is stored on disk and returned on detail view.
form.metadataJsonHelp: Optional JSON metadata stored alongside the skill.

page.title: Channel management
page.description: Manage messaging channel records and their runtime setup.

channels.searchPlaceholder: Search by channel name, kind, or status
channels.emptyChannels: No channels match the current search.
channels.createDescription: Create a channel record and prepare its runtime settings.
channels.editDescription: Review channel metadata and update runtime configuration.
channels.createHelper: Configure the channel profile first. Credentials are collected with typed fields and sent as JSON to match the backend contract.

table.selectAll: Select all channels on this page
table.name: Name

form.name: Channel name
form.requireMentionHelp: Only respond when the bot is explicitly mentioned.
form.enableTypingHelp: Allow the runtime to show typing activity while the agent is responding.
form.allowedUserIdsPlaceholder: Press Enter to add a user id
form.allowedUserIdsHelp: Restrict the channel to specific users. Leave empty to allow everyone.
form.credentialsPreserved: Existing credentials are kept until you enable replacement.
form.replaceCredentialsHelp: Enable this before sending a new credentials payload.
form.telegramTokenHelp: Paste the Telegram token that will be serialized as `token`.
form.discordTokenHelp: Paste the Discord bot token that will be serialized as `token`.
form.slackBotTokenHelp: This becomes `bot_token` in the backend payload.
form.slackAppTokenHelp: This becomes `app_token` in the backend payload.
form.whatsappCreateHelp: Create the WhatsApp channel first, then open the edit page to scan its QR code.
form.whatsappEditHelp: Use the QR panel below to connect this WhatsApp channel by scanning the latest code.
form.noCredentialsRequired: This channel kind does not require manual credentials in this form.
form.routingTargetHelp: Choose whether this channel routes messages to an Agent Team (with orchestrator) or directly to a single Agent.
form.targetTeamHelp: Messages are handled by the team orchestrator and its agents.
form.targetAgentHelp: Messages are routed directly to this agent, bypassing the team orchestrator.

qr.description: Scan this code from WhatsApp to link the channel runtime.
qr.help: If the QR code expires, refresh or restart the channel runtime to request a new one.
